project.name: cube-cos-api
project.description: Central communication API for CubeCOS cluster nodes — manages firmware, health monitoring, node operations, metrics, and integrations using MDNS peer discovery

nodes.name: main.go
nodes.summary: Application entry point — loads config, creates HTTP server, and starts the go-micro service

nodes.name: service.go
nodes.summary: Microservice lifecycle management — creates go-micro service with rate limiting, circuit breaker, registry, and operator orchestration

nodes.name: config.go
nodes.summary: Configuration loading and management — reads YAML config files and syncs global system options

nodes.name: runtime.go
nodes.summary: Runtime initialization — combines server creation and dependency initialization

nodes.name: router.go
nodes.summary: HTTP router setup — creates Gin router, registers all API handlers by node role, sets up middleware

nodes.name: dependency.go
nodes.summary: External dependency initialization — sets up MongoDB, InfluxDB, OpenStack, Keycloak, Kubernetes, AWS clients

nodes.name: identity.go
nodes.summary: Node identity resolution — determines node role, hostname, datacenter, VIP, and authentication setup

nodes.name: filter.go
nodes.summary: Request filtering middleware — authentication and authorization checks for API requests

nodes.name: parse.go
nodes.summary: Request parsing utilities — extracts common request parameters and validates inputs

nodes.name: options.go
nodes.summary: Server options configuration — HTTP server options and settings

nodes.name: log.go
nodes.summary: Logging setup — configures structured logging for the runtime

nodes.name: time.go
nodes.summary: Time utilities for runtime operations

nodes.name: promptmsg.go
nodes.summary: Prompt message constants for user-facing notifications

nodes.name: api.go
nodes.summary: Central handler registry — routes API handlers to node roles (control, compute, storage, moderator, edge-core)

nodes.name: doc.go
nodes.summary: Swagger documentation endpoint registration for API docs

nodes.name: req.go
nodes.summary: Request body reader utility — reads raw body from Gin context

nodes.summary: Query parameter parser utilities — pagination, time ranges, keyword filtering, aggregation

nodes.summary: Watch query parameter parser for SSE streaming endpoints

nodes.summary: Auto-generated Swagger API specification in JSON format

layers.name: Runtime Infrastructure
layers.description: Server initialization, dependency setup, routing, authentication middleware, and microservice lifecycle

layers.name: API Handlers
layers.description: REST API endpoint handlers organized by domain module — request parsing, validation, response formatting, and SSE streaming

layers.name: System Interaction (CubeCOS)
layers.description: Business logic layer wrapping CLI tools (hex_sdk, hex_config), external service clients (InfluxDB, MongoDB, OpenStack, Ceph, Keycloak, S3), and OS-level operations

layers.name: Background Operators
layers.description: Async background workers using Kubernetes workqueues, filesystem watchers, and periodic sync loops for long-running tasks

layers.name: Domain Definitions
layers.description: Domain type definitions, DTOs, constants, error types, and shared data structures used across all layers

layers.name: API Documentation
layers.description: Auto-generated Swagger/OpenAPI documentation for the REST API

layers.name: Build & CI/CD
layers.description: CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions, Jenkins), Docker build environments, RPM packaging, and task automation

layers.name: Deployment & Configuration
layers.description: Systemd service definitions, RPM specs, application config templates, and Go module definition

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: Project documentation — README, architecture docs, API docs, developer guides, and community files

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand CubeCOS API's purpose: it's the central communication mechanism for CubeCOS cluster nodes, using MDNS for peer discovery across control, compute, and storage roles.

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: The main function loads configuration, creates an HTTP server via the runtime package, and starts a go-micro microservice with rate limiting and circuit breaking.

tour.title: Runtime & Server Setup
tour.description: The runtime package initializes external dependencies (MongoDB, InfluxDB, OpenStack, Keycloak, Kubernetes), resolves node identity/role, and sets up the Gin HTTP router with authentication middleware.

tour.title: Role-Based Handler Registration
tour.description: The API registry maps handler modules to node roles (control, compute, storage, moderator, edge-core). Each node only registers handlers appropriate for its role, enabling distributed API responsibilities.

tour.title: API Handler Pattern
tour.description: Each handler module follows a consistent pattern: handlers.go defines route registrations, helper.go encapsulates business logic, with shared request/response utilities for pagination, filtering, and SSE streaming.

tour.title: System Interaction Layer (CubeCOS)
tour.description: The cubecos package wraps all external system interactions — CLI tools (hex_sdk, hex_config), InfluxDB queries, MongoDB operations, OpenStack APIs, Ceph commands, and SSH for cross-node communication.

tour.title: Background Operators
tour.description: Operators implement the service.Operator interface and run as background goroutines. They use Kubernetes workqueues for async task processing and filesystem watchers for reactive state sync.

tour.title: Domain Definitions
tour.description: The definition package provides all domain types, DTOs, constants, and error definitions shared across layers — from node roles and status state machines to metric models and API schemas.

tour.title: Authentication & Authorization
tour.description: The auth layer supports both OIDC (via Keycloak) and SAML 2.0 for SSO. Token creation flows through Keycloak, while request filtering validates auth on every API call.

tour.title: Build & Deployment
tour.description: The project uses GitHub Actions for CI, Jenkins for binary/RPM builds, Docker for build environments, and systemd for production deployment. Taskfile.yaml automates local development tasks.
